title: hellenia sopuensis
intro: Hellenia sopuensis, also known as Helleborus sopuensis, is a rare and endemic species of flowering plant that belongs to the buttercup family, Ranunculaceae. It is found in the Pacific island of Samoa, where it grows in the mountainous regions. The plant has been traditionally used in Samoan medicine, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Hellenia sopuensis include reducing inflammation, treating wounds, and aiding in the recovery from illnesses, and it can be prepared as a herbal tea or infused oil, with some herbalists also using it in tincture form.
